meta.title: The Hollow Forest
meta.author: AWB_
meta.tone: Gothic horror. The forest is hollowed out — something has hollowed it. Cold prose, slow dread, careful hope. Curses are real and cure is hard. Mercy lives in chapels by day; hunger lives in towers by night.

human.summary: You are warm. You bleed. You age. Most things in this story would prefer you stop being any of those.

revenant.summary: You woke in the graveyard with grave-dirt on your tongue. The cold doesn't bite. Neither do most things.

child_of_the_wolf.summary: Born under a long moon. You knew the forest before the forest was hollowed. The change is already in you.

chapel.name: The Chapel of the Pale Bell
chapel.summary: A small stone chapel, half-roofed, candle-stubs guttering on the altar. A font of clear water stands by the door. The priest is here when the sun is up. The bell-tower stairs climb behind the altar; the path south leads down to the village.
example_descriptions: The chapel is cold but clean. Beeswax and old smoke. Whatever the bell rings for, it has not rung lately.
Light through the broken east window paints the floor. The font's water is clear as truth.

down.message: The trapdoor is bolted from the inside. The priest opens it only for those he means to teach.

bell_tower.name: The Bell Tower
bell_tower.summary: A narrow stone tower above the chapel. A great cracked bronze bell hangs in the rafters. The wind through the louvers carries every voice from the village below. A scratched verse runs around the rim.
example_descriptions: The bell is silent. It has not rung in years; the rope is old and grey.
From the louvers you can see the lake to the south, dark even at noon.

graveyard.name: The Old Graveyard
graveyard.summary: Slate stones in long rows, half of them tilted and unreadable. A fresh trench at the back is empty and waiting. A heavy iron grate at the centre opens to a flight of stairs going down — sealed with a tarnished silver lock.
example_descriptions: Mist clings low to the stones. Something pale moved at the corner of your eye and is not there now.
The newest stones are dated within the last year. The names are family names — all of them.

down.message: The crypt grate is sealed by a tarnished silver lock. You'd need a silver key — and the lock takes the key.

crypt.name: The Sealed Crypt
crypt.summary: A barrel-vaulted stone chamber under the graveyard. The dead lie in alcoves on three sides. Something is breathing here that should not be. A silver-tipped dagger rests on a stone slab — undisturbed for a long time.
example_descriptions: Your torch is small in this dark. The shadows move when you don't watch them.
On the slab the dagger gleams: not iron, not steel — silvered, etched with a verse you can't read.

village_square.name: The Village Square
village_square.summary: A cobbled square ringed by shuttered houses. By day, a few villagers move quickly between doors. By night, every shutter is locked from the inside. A stone fountain runs slow with cold water.
example_descriptions: The square is empty in a way that feels rehearsed. Faces flicker behind shutters and do not come out.
The watchman's lantern carves the dark, somewhere down a side street.

smith.name: The Smithy
smith.summary: A low forge of black brick, banked low when the smith is gone. Iron stock racks line one wall; a rack of fine silver-stained chisels lines the other. The smith works only between dusk and midnight — by day, the doors are barred from inside.
example_descriptions: Coals breathing slow under their ash. The smith's hammer rests on the anvil like a sleeping cat.
Bottles of pale-blue oil stand on a shelf. Silvering oil — you've heard rumours of what it does to the wrong wolf.

forest_path.name: The Hollow Path
forest_path.summary: An old cart-track winds south between black firs. Branches knit overhead so the path is lit only by sky-strips. By day, jays scold from the branches. By night, the jays are gone.
example_descriptions: Bracken and pine-mast. The trees lean inward as if listening.
Tracks in the loam — large pads, longer than a wolf's. Not a wolf's.

west.message: The pack-path bends sideways every time you look at it. As a man you can't go in.

black_lake.name: The Black Lake
black_lake.summary: A still oval of water, dark even by daylight. A stone tower rises from the far shore — one window lit, always lit, never the same window twice. A path leads up to the tower. A submerged stairwell descends below the boathouse.
example_descriptions: The lake does not reflect the sky. It reflects only what stands close enough to see itself in it.
The tower seems closer than it is, then farther. You stop trying to measure.

count_tower.name: The Count's Tower
count_tower.summary: A circular chamber high in the lake-tower. Velvet drapes, a long table, two chairs. The Count is here only between the late hours and the first grey of dawn. By day, the tower is empty — and yet you feel watched anyway.
example_descriptions: The drapes are dust-free. The wine in the decanter is fresh. The chair across the table is — exactly — pulled out.
From the high window the village looks like a coin tossed into the woods.

moonlit_cellar.name: The Moonlit Cellar
moonlit_cellar.summary: Below the boathouse the water gives way to a low stone cellar. Pale light comes from above through cracked floorboards — pale even at noon. The walls are old; the marks on them are older.
example_descriptions: Dripping echoes. A soft scratching from somewhere you can't place.
Bottles in racks. The labels are blank. The corks are sealed with wax stamped with a stranger's sign.

chapel_undercroft.name: The Chapel Undercroft
chapel_undercroft.summary: Stone shelves of bottles, dried herbs in bundles. The brewing-bench is here, where the priest teaches the work to those he believes can drink the cure. A small fire burns in a covered grate.
example_descriptions: The air is dry and cool. Candles in iron sconces. The brew smells of crushed herb and clean water.
A book lies open on the bench — the cure-recipe, copied in the priest's careful hand.

wolf_den.name: The Wolf Den
wolf_den.summary: A hollow at the foot of two leaning firs. The earth here is packed flat by long use. Bones, picked clean. The pack sleeps here when the moon is wrong; the alpha sleeps further in.
example_descriptions: The air is hot, thick with breath. They have been here. They will be again.
A path goes deeper into the brush — west, where no human ever went on two legs.

west.message: The path bends in a way only paws can follow. As a man you cannot find it.

the_hollow.name: The Hollow
the_hollow.summary: A perfect circle of trees, all leaning the same way. The moon is always above this clearing, even at noon. In the centre, a stone, and on the stone, the alpha — first wolf, oldest wolf, the one the forest hollowed itself for. She is waiting.
example_descriptions: No wind. The moon does not move. The alpha breathes slow and old.
The centre stone is warm. Somebody knelt here, once. Somebody made a wolf, once.

east.message: You cannot leave this place as a man. You must finish what you came as.

rusty_dagger.description: A workshop dagger — pitted, but it holds an edge.

silver_dagger.description: A long dagger with a silvered tip and a verse etched along the blade.

silver_oil.description: A pale blue oil. Anointing a blade with it makes the blade bite the cursed.

silver_dust.description: Fine, cold to the touch. Useful in oil-making.

silver_key.description: A small key, tarnished black. Cold even in your hand.

holy_water.description: Drawn from the chapel font and blessed. Restores life to the warm; burns the cursed.

wolfsbane.description: A pale herb with violet flowers. Bitter on the tongue. The forest knows it.

moonsilver_herb.description: A grey-leafed herb that grows only on consecrated ground. Soft as cobweb.

garlic.description: A pungent bulb. Folk wisdom claims more than it can prove. Some folk wisdom is right.

torch.description: A pitch-soaked torch. Burns warm, burns short.

bone_relic.description: A small carved finger-bone, tied with red thread. Lucky, the smith claims.

raw_meat.description: Stringy, dark, cooling fast. Better than nothing — barely. Best eaten before it turns.

wood.description: Branch wood. Dry enough to burn, dense enough to whittle.

silver_blessing.description: Distilled wolfsbane and moonsilver in a vial of holy water. Tastes like winter morning. Said to lift the curse from the cursed.

count_signet.description: A heavy ring of black silver, marked with a sign no living priest will name.

wolf_pelt.description: A heavy fur, taken in the dark. The hunter pays for these.

relic_of_origin.description: A small carved tooth on a sinew thong. The first cursed thing the forest ever knew. Cold, even in your hand.

foraging.description: Pick herbs, gather small things from the underbrush.

alchemy.description: Distill silver oil; brew the blessing if you have what is needed.

smithing.description: The smith's craft — coax metal, set silver into edge.

lycanthropy.description: The wolf's gift, unasked-for. While you carry it, you can `transform wolf` — fast, fanged, and unwelcome. Dawn ends the change. The priest can lift the curse.

vampirism.description: The Count's gift, asked for. While you carry it, you can `transform bat` — fast, healing on the bite. Sunlight burns whether you're shaped or not. There is no cure.

hunting.description: Track and engage hostile things. The forest has not been short of them lately.

firemaking.description: Coax a flame from kindling. Needed for forge work, brewing, and most cold nights.

distill_silver_oil.description: Distill silver dust into oil. The smith pays for it; the cursed fear it.

brew_blessing.description: Distill wolfsbane + moonsilver in holy water. The cure for the curse — if the cursed will drink it.

forge_silver_dagger.description: Forge a silvered edge onto a rusty dagger. The smith will do it for you, or you can do it yourself if you've learned the work.

werewolf.summary: A wolf, broken-shaped. Larger than it should be. The eyes are not a wolf's eyes. The bite is hot.

revenant.summary: A villager, weeks dead, walking. They do not know you. They do not know themselves.

bat_swarm.summary: A column of dark, moving as one. Hard to see; harder to hit. The cellar is theirs.

shade.summary: A tall, thin shape that bends light wrong. The crypt does not want you here.

grave_dog.summary: A long, lean dog living in the graveyard. It does not bark. It works.

alpha_wolf.summary: Massive, white-eyed, older than the village. The first thing the forest hollowed itself for. It does not run.

bell_riddle.prompt: I ring at dawn for those who wake. I ring at dusk for those who don't. I have never rung for the one who waits in the lake. What am I?
bell_riddle.on_solve_message: The verse on the rim catches the morning light, and you read it for the first time. Beneath it, scratched in a different hand: 'Silver waits where silver lay — the grave does not turn the silver away.'

the_priest.summary: A thin, careful man in grey vestments. He is not afraid of you, and you can see he has reason to be.
dialogue_seeds: Sit by the font if you need to. The water is clean.
We do not ring the bell any more. Some sounds find ears we'd rather they didn't.
Whatever you've heard about what stays in the lake at night — believe it.

lines: I see it in your eyes. The change is in you. Don't lie to me — I've seen it before.
There is a way back. Wolfsbane in the forest. Moonsilver in the graveyard. Holy water from this font. Bring them. I'll teach you the work.
You should not be here. Not in the day. Not in this house.
There is no cure for what you have done. Only mercy left, and not from me.
I have heard the village speak of you. A name in the hollow forest is a rare thing.
Sit. You are welcome here as long as you carry no curse.
What brings you here, traveller?

choices.label: I think I have been bitten.

lines: We were a hundred souls a year ago. We are forty now. Most went into the graveyard. A few — went into the woods.
The watchman walks the round still. He carries the silver key for the crypt, when he remembers to.
The Count came thirty years ago. The lake was a pond then. After he came, the pond grew dark, then deep, then — what it is.
Do not go up to the tower at night. Or do, if you've made your peace. He does not lie to those who climb.
Yes. I see. There is a way. Wolfsbane in the forest, moonsilver among the stones, this water in your hand. Bring them. I will teach you the brewing.
If you bring me back the silver blessing — and you have the courage to drink it — the curse breaks.
You have it. Give it to me. Drink. Hold still. ...
It is done. The wolf has left you. Do not look for it again.

effects.text: >>> Your blood quiets. The hunger is gone. You feel only tired.

the_smith.summary: A short, broad woman with arms like cable and a dark patience. She works late, sleeps later.
dialogue_seeds: The forge runs late. The forge runs warm. Bring me work I can finish.
Silver oil's in the rack. Three vials and a rusty knife and I'll silver it for you.

the_watchman.summary: An old man with a brass-handled lantern, walking the round. He nods. He never stops walking.
dialogue_seeds: Stay in by dark, traveller. The lake's bell rings for nobody now.
I carry the crypt's key, when I remember it. Some days I do.

the_count.summary: Tall, pale, courteous. He stands when you enter. He pours for you. The wine is excellent.

lines: So. You climbed. Few do. Most who climb are only curious.
Tell me, then — why are you here?
Curious is honest, at least. Sit. Drink, if you like. The wine will not bite you.
I have been here longer than the village. I will be here longer than the village. The lake came with me; the lake will go with me.

choices.label: What do you want from me?

lines: From you? Almost nothing. A guest is a guest. I want only that one of every hundred who climbs the tower decides, on their own, to stay.
When you decide, you will know. You will come back. Or you won't. Either is acceptable.
You're sure?
It is permanent. The sun will not love you. The mortal water will burn. But you will be patient. You will be many things. You will be — yourself, but more.

choices.label: (But you are already cursed — the wolf would not allow it.)
I'm not sure. Tell me more.

lines: Then take my hand. Hold still.
It is done. Welcome, child. The night is long, and now it is yours.

effects.text: >>> Your blood goes still. The cold tastes of wine. You will not need to eat the way you did.

lines: Ah. Silver. I see. You came prepared.
I will not fight you, traveller. I am very old. If you must, do it. I would only ask you take this — proof, for the priest, that the village is free.

effects.text: >>> The Count slips a heavy ring from his finger and sets it on the table.

lines: ...so it ends. Thank you.

effects.text: >>> The silver bites where silver bites. He goes still. The lake outside ripples once and is calm.

lines: I cannot give what you already have. The wolf is in you, and the wolf is jealous.
Bring me a man, and I might bring back a count. But not before.
They speak of you below, traveller. Even my bats carry the rumour up.
Few who climb to me have a name yet. You have one. Sit.

the_hunter.summary: A weathered tracker who came up from the south the year the forest hollowed. She works the wolf-paths and keeps the village fed in pelts.
dialogue_seeds: Bring me pelts. Three buys you a fair coin.
There's an old wolf at the heart of the wood. Older than the rest. She is what started it.
Walk careful by the lake at night. The tower hears.
Standing rewards: thirty gold for any pelt you bring me, more for the alpha. If you want to make it official — type 'bounty post werewolf 30' — and the village will know.

intro_chapel.title: The Hollow Forest
intro_chapel.description: You woke at the chapel. The priest will tell you what little he knows. Talk with him; visit the village; choose your path.
intro_chapel.completion_message: You have your bearings. The priest watches by day, the Count waits by night. The forest is yours to choose.

the_curse.title: The Curse
the_curse.description: You've been bitten. The priest offers a cure: bring wolfsbane (forest), moonsilver (graveyard), holy water (this font), and learn the brewing. Drink the silver blessing and the wolf leaves you.
the_curse.completion_message: The priest takes the blessing from your hand. 'Now drink — and hold still.' (Talk to the priest with `talk priest` to be cleansed; pick the cleansing branch.)

the_gift.title: The Gift
the_gift.description: You have accepted the Count's invitation. He asks one small thing: prove the gift is in you. Feed the hunger three times.
the_gift.completion_message: The Count receives you again. 'Three is enough. The night accepts you.' He pours another glass.

the_slayer.title: The Slayer
the_slayer.description: Some villages do not need a count. Forge a silver-tipped dagger, climb the tower at night, and end this. The smith will help if you bring her silver oil.
the_slayer.completion_message: You bring the priest the signet. He weeps, briefly, and rings the bell himself. The shutters in the village open one by one.

the_silver_key.title: The Silver Key
the_silver_key.description: Climb the bell-tower. Read the verse on the rim. Solve what the bell asks — and the silver lock will yield.
the_silver_key.completion_message: The key is yours. The crypt waits.

pelt_bounty.title: Pelt Bounty
pelt_bounty.description: The hunter pays in coin for wolf pelts — three at a time. Bring her three to start.
pelt_bounty.completion_message: She weighs them, nods once, and pays. "Bring more if you find them."

hollow_origin.title: The Hollow Origin
hollow_origin.description: The hunter speaks of an alpha at the heart of the wood — the first wolf, the one the forest hollowed itself for. Find the relic she wears. Bring it back.
hollow_origin.completion_message: She takes the relic, holds it long enough that you wonder, and gives it back. "It belongs to whoever ends her. That was you. Keep it."

the_lost_sister.title: The Lost Sister
the_lost_sister.description: The priest mentioned a name once: a young woman of the village who walked into the graveyard last winter and did not come back. Find a relic of hers, bring it to him for closure.
the_lost_sister.completion_message: He weeps, briefly, and sets the relic on the altar. "Thank you. The family will come tonight."

effects.text: >>> The bite burns deeper than a bite should. Something passes from its mouth into yours.
[The sun bites your skin where it touches.]
>>> The villagers do not look up. You are not the strangest thing this year.

blood_moon.title: Blood Moon
blood_moon.description: The moon is full and red over the lake. Werewolves rage tonight; the Count is twice as patient.

narration.on_start: [A red moon climbs over the lake. The forest exhales. Werewolves move tonight, and not as wolves do.]
narration.on_complete: >>> The pack grows quiet around you. The moon dims a shade. The village will whisper of you again.
narration.on_expire: [The moon pales toward dawn. Whatever passed, passed without you.]
